1.1: PAUL and Ti-mo’the-us, servants of Jesus Christ, to all the saints in Jesus Christ who are at Philippi, together with the elders and deacons:
1.2: Grace be to you, and peace from God our Father, and from our LORD Jesus Christ.
1.3: I give thanks to my God for your steady remembrance of me.
1.4: In all my prayers for you, I make supplication with joy,
1.5: For your fellowship in the gospel, from the very first day until now,
1.6: Being confident of this very thing, that he who has begun the good work among you, the same will continue it until the day of our LORD Jesus Christ:
1.7: And this is the right way for me to think of you all, because I have you in my heart, because through all my imprisonment and my defense and confirmation of the truth of the gospel, you have been partakers with me of grace.
1.8: For God is my witness of how much I love you through the love of Jesus Christ.
1.9: And for this I pray, that your love may abound yet more and more in knowledge and in all spiritual understanding:
1.10: So that you may choose the things that are excellent; and that you may be pure and without offence in the day of Christ;
1.11: And be filled with the fruits of righteousness, which are by Jesus Christ, to the glory and the praise of God.
1.12: Now I would have you know this, my brethren, that my work has been greatly furthered by the gospel;
1.13: And the reasons for my imprisonments have been made manifest by Christ to all Cæsar’s court, and to all men.
1.14: And many of the brethren in our LORD have grown confident by my imprisonment and, with increasing boldness, speak the word of God without fear.
1.15: While some of them preach only because of envy and strife; others preach Christ in good will and love:
1.16: For they know that I am appointed for the defence of the gospel:
1.17: But those who preach Christ out of contention, do it not sincerely, but do it expecting to increase the hardship of my imprisonment.
1.18: And I have rejoiced and still do rejoice in this: that in every way, whether in pretense or in truth, Christ is preached.
1.19: For I know that through your prayers and the gift of the Spirit of Jesus Christ, all these things will ultimately turn out for my salvation,
1.20: Just as it is my earnest hope and expectation that in nothing shall I be ashamed, but that openly as always, so also now will Christ be magnified through my body, whether in life or death.
1.21: For Christ is my life, and to die is gain.
1.22: Even if, in this life of the flesh, my labors bear fruits, I do not know what to choose.
1.23: For I am drawn between two desires, the one to depart, that I may be with Christ, which is far better:
1.24: Nevertheless, for me to remain in the flesh is more needful for you.
1.25: And this I surely know, that I shall be spared and remain for your joy and for the furtherance of your faith;
1.26: So that when I come again to you, your rejoicing in Jesus Christ will abound through me.
1.27: Only conduct yourselves as becomes the gospel of Christ: so that whether I come and see you, or whether I am far away, I may hear of your good conduct, that you are standing firm in one spirit, and in one soul, and triumphing together through the faith of the gospel;
1.28: And that in nothing are you terrified by our adversaries, whose conduct is the sign of their own destruction, but your salvation, and this is from God.
1.29: For it has been given to you, not only to believe in Christ, but also to suffer for his sake.
1.30: And that you may endure such trials as those which you have seen me in, and such as you now hear that I am in.

2.1: IF, therefore, you have found consola tion in Christ, or whole hearted love or fellowship of the Spirit or compassion and mercies,
2.2: Complete my joy by being in one accord and one love and one soul and one mind.
2.3: Do nothing through strife or vain glory; but in humility let each regard his neighbor better than himself.
2.4: Let no one be mindful only of his own things, but let every one be mindful of the things of his neighbor also.
2.5: Reason this within you which Jesus Christ also reasoned,
2.6: Who, being in the form of God, did not consider it robbery to be equal with God:
2.7: But made himself of no reputation and took upon himself the form of a servant, and was in the likeness of men:
2.8: And, being found in fashion as a man, he humbled himself, and became obedient to death, even the death of the cross.
2.9: Wherefore God also has highly exalted him, and given him a name which is above every name;
2.10: That at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, of those in heaven, of those on earth, and those under the earth,
2.11: And every tongue shall confess that Jesus Christ is the LORD, to the glory of God his Father.
2.12: From now on, my beloved, just as you have always been obedient, not only in my presence, but much more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling.
2.13: For it is God who inspires you with the will to do the good things which you desire to do.
2.14: Do all things without disputing, and doubting:
2.15: That you may be sincere and blameless, like the innocent children of God, in the midst of a crooked and perverse generation, among whom you shine as lights in the world:
2.16: For you are to them the light of life; for my pride and glory in the day of Christ, for I have not run in vain nor labored in vain.
2.17: Yes, even if my blood be offered upon the sacrifice and the service of your faith, I am happy and rejoice with you all.
2.18: Likewise you also must be happy and rejoice with me.
2.19: But I trust in our LORD Jesus Christ to send Ti-mo’the-us to you soon, that I also may be at ease when I learn of your well being.
2.20: For I have no one here as interested as I am, who will sincerely care for your welfare;
2.21: For all seek their own, not the things which are Jesus Christ’s.
2.22: But you know his record, that as a son with his father, he has served with me in the gospel.
2.23: I hope to send him to you presently, as soon as I see how it will go with me.
2.24: But I trust in my LORD that I also myself shall come shortly.
2.25: But right now I am forced through circumstances to send to you Epaphroditus, a brother, and assistant and co-worker with me, but he is also your apostle and one who ministers to my wants.
2.26: For he has been longing to see you all, and has been depressed because he knew you had heard that he had been sick.
2.27: For indeed he was sick to the point of death but God had mercy on him; and not on him only, but on me also, lest I should have sorrow upon sorrow.
2.28: Therefore I have sent him quickly, so that, when you see him again, you may rejoice, and that I may be relieved from anxiety.
2.29: Welcome him, therefore, in the LORD with all joy; and honour those who are like him:
2.30: Because for the work of Christ he came near to death; and by his self denial, he made good your lack of service to me.

3.1: HENCEFORTH, my brethren, rejoice in our LORD. It does not bother me to write the same things to you, because they enlighten you.
3.2: Beware of backbiters, beware of evil workers, beware of circumcising.
3.3: For we are the true people of circumcision, who worship God in Spirit, and glory in Jesus Christ, and yet do not rely on things of the flesh.
3.4: As for me, I once relied on things of the flesh. However, if a man thinks his hope is on things of the flesh, I have more hope than he has;
3.5: Because I was circumcised when I was eight days old, being an Israelite by race, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew son of Hebrews, and according to the law a Pharisee;
3.6: And because, concerning zeal, I was a persecutor of the church; and according to the standards of righteousness of the law, I was blameless.
3.7: But these things which once were a gain to me, I counted a loss for the sake of Christ.
3.8: And I still count them all a loss, for the sake of abundant knowledge of Jesus Christ my LORD: for whom I have lost everything, and I have considered all those things as refuse, so that I may increase in Christ
3.9: And be found in him, since I have no righteousness of my own gained from the law, but the righteousness which comes through the faith of Christ; that is, the righteousness which comes from God:
3.10: So that through this righteousness I may know Jesus and the power of his resurrection, and be a partaker of his sufferings, even to a death like his;
3.11: That I may by any means attain the resurrection from the dead.
3.12: Not as though I had already attained or were already perfect; but I am striving, that I may reach that for which Jesus Christ appointed me.
3.13: My brethren, I do not consider that I have reached the goal; but this one thing I do know, forgetting those things which are behind me, I strive for those things which are before me,
3.14: I press toward the goal to receive the prize of victory of God’s highest calling through Jesus Christ.
3.15: Therefore let those of you who are perfect think these things over; and if you reason in any other way, God will reveal even that to you.
3.16: Nevertheless, whereto we have already attained, let us walk by the same path and with one accord.
3.17: My brethren, be followers like me, and observe those who walk such a path, and then you will be examples as we are.
3.18: For there are many who live otherwise, of whom I have often told you, and now I tell you with tears that they are the enemies of the cross of Christ;
3.19: Whose end is destruction, whose God is their belly, and whose glory is in their shame; whose thought is on earthly things.
3.20: But our labours are in heavenly things, from whence we look for our Saviour, our LORD Jesus Christ,
3.21: Who shall transform our poor body, to the likeness of his glorious body, according to his mighty power, whereby he is able even to subdue all things unto himself.

4.1: HENCEFORTH, my dearly beloved brethren, my joy and crown, in this manner stand firm in our LORD, my beloved.
4.2: I beseech Euodias and I beseech Syntyche to be of one accord in our LORD.
4.3: I beseech you also, my true yokefellow, help those women who laboured with me in the gospel, together with Clement, and with the rest of my fellowlabourers, whose names are written in the book of life.
4.4: Rejoice in our LORD alway; and again I say, Rejoice.
4.5: Let your humility be known to all men. Our LORD is at hand.
4.6: Do not worry over things; but always by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known to God.
4.7: And the peace of God, which passes all understanding, shall keep your hearts and minds through Jesus Christ.
4.8: Finally, my brethren, whatsoever things are true, whatsoever things are honest, whatsoever things are just, whatsoever things are pure, whatsoever things are lovely, whatsoever things are of good report; if there be any virtue, and if there be any praise, think on these things.
4.9: Those things, which you have learned and received, and heard, and seen in me, do: and the God of peace shall be with you.
4.10: But I rejoiced in our LORD greatly, that you have continued to care for me, just as you have always cared, even though you yourselves have not had sufficient.
4.11: Nor am I saying this simply because I am in want; for I have learned to make what I have meet my needs.
4.12: I know what it is to be poor, and I know what it is to be rich: I have gone through many things and experienced many things, both to be full and to be hungry, both to have plenty and to be in want.
4.13: I can do all things through Christ who strengthens me.
4.14: But you have done well to share my difficulties.
4.15: Now you Philippians know also, that in the beginning of the gospel, when I departed from Mac-e-do’ni-a, no church shared with me, as concerning giving and receiving, but you only.
4.16: For even at Thessalonica you sent more than once to meet my needs.
4.17: I do not say this because I want a gift, but because I want to see the fruits of the gospel increased to you.
4.18: I have received everything I need, and it is more than enough: I am satisfied, having received everything you sent me by Epaphroditus, and it was welcome as a fragrant perfume and a sacrifice acceptable and well pleasing to God.
4.19: But my God will supply all your needs according to his riches in the glory of Jesus Christ.
4.20: Now unto God our Father be glory and honour, for ever and ever. Amen.
4.21: Salute every saint in Jesus Christ. The brethren who are with me greet you.
4.22: All the saints salute you, especially those who are of Cæsar’s household.
4.23: The grace of our LORD Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
